introSection: Thunderbolt is a steel roller coaster located at Luna Park at Coney Island in Brooklyn, New York, USA. The ride was built by Zamperla and features four inversions. It is situated on the plot of the former Thunderbolt, a wooden roller coaster which opened in 1925, closed in 1982, and was demolished in 2000.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The ride was originally planned to open on May 22, 2014 but was delayed.

Thunderbolt opened on June 14, 2014. Shortly after opening, a trim brake was added after the first airtime hill.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Orange track and white supports. The plot the ride sits on is by.

sections.title: Ride experience
sections.content: The ride begins with a vertical lift hill. After cresting the hill, riders descend a vertical drop and straight into a large vertical loop. The car then enters a zero-g roll, followed by a stengel dive and then a banked curve. The car then enters a dive loop that sends it back towards the station. The car travels through a corkscrew and over two airtime hills before hitting the brake run.

fullText:
Thunderbolt is a steel roller coaster located at Luna Park at Coney Island in Brooklyn, New York, USA. The ride was built by Zamperla and features four inversions. It is situated on the plot of the former Thunderbolt, a wooden roller coaster which opened in 1925, closed in 1982, and was demolished in 2000.

The ride was originally planned to open on May 22, 2014 but was delayed.

Thunderbolt opened on June 14, 2014. Shortly after opening, a trim brake was added after the first airtime hill.

Orange track and white supports. The plot the ride sits on is by.

The ride begins with a vertical lift hill. After cresting the hill, riders descend a vertical drop and straight into a large vertical loop. The car then enters a zero-g roll, followed by a stengel dive and then a banked curve. The car then enters a dive loop that sends it back towards the station. The car travels through a corkscrew and over two airtime hills before hitting the brake run.




 * Ride page on the park's website
